exemplar.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

detailedExemplarData.title: M25 #6: Does acquiring knowledge destroy our sense of wonder? Discuss with reference to two areas of knowledge.

strengths: Consistent and sustained focus on the prescribed title throughout introduction, body and conclusion
Effective use of specific examples from both the arts and the natural sciences to support knowledge claims
Coherent development of arguments with clear logical progression
Awareness of contrasting perspectives (e.g. scientific vs artistic viewpoints) and acknowledgment of counterclaims
weaknesses: Evaluation of alternative viewpoints remains brief and largely descriptive rather than critically analysed
Several analytical threads (e.g. Bach fugue, myth versus science) are under-developed and occasionally lapse into description
Frequent lapses in academic rigour: inconsistent citation formats, unsupported generalizations (e.g. vole-to-human emotion extrapolation)
Transitions and signposting between ideas are uneven, affecting the cohesion and flow of the argument
Critical engagement with examples is sometimes superficial (e.g. lack of methodological critique in the oxytocin study)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although the essay demonstrates a sustained focus on the title, effective examples, and coherent argumentationâfeatures characteristic of the 7â8 bandâthe numerous surface-level analyses, under-exploited lines of thought and persistent academic conventions issues (citations, evidential support, cohesion) weaken its overall critical depth. In line with the official examinerâs total of 6, this performance better aligns with the 5â6 band: the discussion remains focused and supported, but the limited evaluation of counterclaims, descriptive tendencies and structural weaknesses justify a score of 6 out of 10.

annotations.comment: The extrapolation from vole behaviour to human emotions is asserted rather than justified. Acknowledge the limitations of animal models when applying such conclusions to human experiences.
The Bach fugue example vividly explains stretto technique and demonstrates how technical musical knowledge can amplify, rather than destroy, the listenerâs sense of wonder.
When defining âstretto,â include a proper citation and explain why the source is credible to strengthen the academic rigor of this analysis.
Reliance on personal anecdote risks weakening objectivity. Tie the example back to broader patterns in the natural sciences to generalize beyond the individual experience.

The definitions of the two AOKs are simplistic. The student should elaborate on how âtechnical and contextual knowledgeâ in the arts and âobjective explanationâ in the natural sciences uniquely influence wonder.
Some entries include URLs without author/date formatting. Ensure each citation contains full bibliographic details (author, title, source, date) to uphold academic conventions.

The definition of âwonderâ in this paragraph is broad and philosophical but lacks a precise connection to the role of acquiring knowledge. The student should refine the thesis to clearly state how knowledge might destroy or enhance wonder, anchoring the discussion more directly to the essay prompt.

The statement that âdestructionâ of wonder may not always be a detriment introduces nuance but reads as an afterthought. The student should integrate this qualification into the thesis earlier for a more cohesive introduction.

The transition to the first body paragraph feels abrupt. A clear topic sentence signalling the argumentâs direction would strengthen cohesion and guide the reader through the studentâs line of reasoning.
The Kubrick example in The Shining is vivid and specific, effectively illustrating how contextual knowledge can impose ethical constraints that dampen aesthetic wonder.
The ethical implications of Kubrickâs treatment are noted but not critically analysed. The student should evaluate the directorâs rationale versus the moral cost to deepen the counterclaim.
This paragraph draws a strong parallel between aesthetic disillusionment in art and science but could benefit from clearer signposting to underscore the comparative structure of the argument.
The description of the prairie vole oxytocin study is detailed and correctly links biochemical research to the concept of diminishing wonder through a single scientific explanation.
There is little evaluation of the methodology in the oxytocin study. Discuss potential biases or alternative interpretations to strengthen critical analysis of scientific knowledge claims.
This paragraph contrasts ignorant versus informed listeners but lacks evaluation of the counter-perspective. Critically assess whether there are limits to how much analysis can enhance wonder.
The moon example draws effectively on personal cultural background to illustrate how scientific knowledge transforms, rather than diminishes, a sense of wonder.

The final sentence asserts that knowledge will never fully eliminate mystery but lacks support from earlier arguments. Integrate this insight into body paragraphs to build a more cohesive line of reasoning.
The âWorks citedâ heading is correctly placed, but the list that follows shows inconsistent citation formats. Apply a single, recognised style (e.g., MLA or APA) throughout.
The âContactâ citation lacks contextâhow is this novel used in the essay? Clarify in-text references to strengthen the link between sources and analysis.
Hyperlinks in the bibliography are presented without proper formatting. Use angle brackets or a consistent hyperlink style to maintain a formal academic tone.

The conclusion reiterates main points but does not synthesise the implications regarding knowledge and wonder. The student should draw out broader questions or future considerations to deepen the closing.
The repeated italicised prompt on each page is unnecessary. In a final draft, it may be removed to focus the readerâs attention on the analysis rather than on the repeated question.
Consider reducing paragraph length by splitting dense analyses into smaller sections. This will improve readability and sharpen the focus of each point.

The natural sciences section conflates empirical description with personal interpretation. Structure the paragraph to first present the scientific data, then critically reflect on its implications for wonder.
Avoid restating the prompt verbatim at the start of each major section. Instead, use topic sentences that directly preview the upcoming argument.
The paragraph on Kubrick spans a large block of text, absorbing both description and analysis. Separating the factual recounting from the evaluative discussion would enhance clarity.

rubricCriterionStrandBandContents.content:
The essay does not
reach a standard
described by levels
1â5 or is not a
response to one of
the prescribed titles
on the list for the
current session.
The discussion is weakly connected to the title. While there may be links to the areas of knowledge, any relevant points are descriptive or consist only of unsupported assertions.

The discussion is connected to the title and makes superficial or limited links to areas of knowledge. The discussion is largely descriptive. Limited arguments are offered but they are unclear and are not supported by effective examples.

The discussion
is focused on
the title and is
developed with
some links to
areas of
knowledge.
Arguments are
offered and are
supported by
examples.
There is some
awareness of
different points
of view.

The discussion is focused on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and supported by examples. There is awareness and some evaluation of different points of view.

The discussion has a sustained focus on the title and is linked effectively to areas of knowledge. Arguments are clear, coherent and effectively supported by specific examples. The implications of arguments are considered. There is clear awareness and evaluation of different points of view.
